title: Django: Zero to Advanced
description: A complete roadmap for mastering Django backend development, from installation to production-grade patterns.

steps.title: Introduction to Django
steps.description: Overview of what Django is and why it's a powerful choice for backend development. We'll cover the MVC/MVT pattern, Django's philosophy of 'batteries included', and what you'll build by the end of this journey.

steps.title: Setup & Project Structure
steps.description: Install Python and Django, create your first project with `django-admin startproject`, and understand the anatomy of a Django project — `settings.py`, `urls.py`, `wsgi.py`, and the app structure.

steps.title: Models & ORM
steps.description: Define data models using Django's ORM, run migrations with `makemigrations` and `migrate`, and understand field types, relationships (`ForeignKey`, `ManyToManyField`), and the Django admin interface.

steps.title: Views & URLs
steps.description: Build function-based views (FBVs) and class-based views (CBVs), configure URL routing with `urlpatterns`, pass context to templates, and understand the request/response cycle.

steps.title: QuerySets & Lookups
steps.description: Master QuerySet operations: `filter()`, `exclude()`, `annotate()`, `aggregate()`, `values()`, and `only()`. Learn field lookups (`__gt`, `__contains`, `__in`) and how to chain QuerySets.

steps.title: Performance Optimization
steps.description: Use `select_related()` for ForeignKey joins and `prefetch_related()` for ManyToMany to eliminate N+1 query problems. Profile queries with Django Debug Toolbar and use `explain()` to analyze query plans.

steps.title: DRF: Django REST Framework
steps.description: Build REST APIs with Django REST Framework — serializers, viewsets, routers, authentication classes (Token, JWT), and permission policies. Connect to a React or mobile frontend.
